raw_label_text: REFINED WHEAT FLOUR (MAIDA) (60%), EDIBLE VEGETABLE OIL (PALMOLEIN, RICE BRAN), TAPIOCA STARCH, CORN FLOUR, EDIBLE COMMON SALT, DEHYDRATED VEGETABLE POWDER (ONION, GARLIC, TAMARIND), SPICES & CONDIMENTS 2% (DRY MANGO, CHILLI, PEPPER, CUMIN), SUGAR, YEAST EXTRACT, ACIDITY REGULATOR (INS 297, INS 261, INS 330), NATURAL & NATURE IDENTICAL FLAVOURING SUBSTANCES (MASALA), FLAVOUR ENHANCER (INS 635, INS 620), ANTICAKING AGENT (INS 554, INS 501(ii) (III)), PAPRIKA EXTRACT (INS 140).

ground_truth_status.notes: INS 501(ii) (Potassium hydrogen carbonate) is a raising agent and acidity regulator; it does not possess anticaking properties. Additionally, while INS 140 (Chlorophylls) is a colour, it is incorrectly identified as Paprika Extract (which is INS 160c), but this was not flagged as a functional class mismatch since both belong to the 'Colour' class.

raw_label_text: Sugar, Milk Solids. Fractionated vegetable Fat, Hydrogenated Vegetable fats, Refined Wheat flour (Maida), Cocoa solids (4.5%), Emulsifier (Soya lecithin), Yeast, Raising agent, Artificial flavouring substance (vanilla), Iodised salt, Flour treatment agent (341) and Nature Identical flavoring substance

ground_truth_status.notes: The label contains one INS code: 341 (Calcium phosphates). According to the Codex Alimentarius (CXG 36-1989), INS 341(i), 341(ii), and 341(iii) are all validly classified as Flour Treatment Agents, which matches the declared functional class on the label. Other additives like Soya lecithin (INS 322) are mentioned by name without codes, which is acceptable under the audit rules.

ground_truth_status.notes: All INS codes identified in the label—120 (Carmines), 261 (Potassium acetates), and 401 (Sodium alginate)—are correctly assigned to their declared functional classes (Colour, Acidity Regulator, and Stabilizer respectively) according to Codex Alimentarius.

fssai_category: Condiments, Sauces, Tomato sauces, Ketchup, Tomato Ketchup
raw_label_text: water, tomato paste (28%), sugar, iodised salt, acidity regulator - E297, stabilizers - E440, E402, preservative - E249, onion powder, garlic powder, spices, condiments

ground_truth_status.notes: All identified INS codes (297, 440, 402, 249) are correctly assigned to their respective functional classes according to the Codex Alimentarius. INS 297 (Fumaric acid) is an acidity regulator; INS 440 (Pectins) and INS 402 (Potassium alginate) are stabilizers; and INS 249 (Potassium nitrite) is a preservative.

raw_label_text: WATER, MANGO PULP (19.5%), SUGAR, ACIDITY REGULATOR (290), ANTIOXIDANT(322) AND PRESERVATIVE (222) CONTAINS PERMITTED SYNTHETIC FOOD COLOUR (160c) AND ADDED MANGO FLAVOURS (NATURAL, NATURE-IDENTICAL AND ARTIFICIAL FLAVOURING SUBSTANCES).

ground_truth_status.notes: INS 290 (Carbon dioxide) is not recognized as an acidity regulator under Codex Alimentarius; its primary functions are carbonation and preservation. INS 160c (Paprika extract) is a natural colour, and while the label incorrectly describes it as a 'synthetic food colour', the functional class 'Colour' itself is correct, so it was not flagged as a mismatch.
